title: berkheya viscosa
intro: Berkheya viscosa, also known as sticky bush or sticky yellowhead, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots and leaves being used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. Berkheya viscosa can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
